plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-326 RP10.P04.M02.S08-RP10.P04.M04.S17 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-326 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP10.P04 UI workflow slice: the M02 secondary interaction tail, the M03 primary implementation slice full 22-row UI cycle (UI surfaces, data dependencies, loading/empty/denied/review-required states, interactions, permission badges, audit hint displays, error message copies, responsive layouts, keyboard/focus behavior, visual density checks, synthetic fixture bindings, build smokes, Hermes UI evidence, Claude UI leak prompts, closeout handoffs, state snapshots, unauthorized count leak guards), and the M04 secondary workflow slice 17-row head across three sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H10), Claude review packet (C10), and CP00-327 handoff, without opening UI runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-326 is a descriptor-only intake-core pack (RP10.P04.M02.S08-RP10.P04.M04.S17, 40 units, risk B) extending the CP00-321 through CP00-325 lineage; it opens no runtime surface. Read-only inspection confirms: (1) coverage is exact — the 40 included_units yield the prompt's deliverable distribution (ui 18, implementation 10, claude_review 3, security_audit 4, fixture 2, test 2, hermes_evidence 1) and micro distribution (M02 Type-And-Shape 1, M03 Primary 22, M04 Secondary 17), with validators.js + model.test.js + validate-rp10-intake-core-contract.mjs enforcing section/row counts and contract<->registry deepEqual; (2) the pack-level production_ready claim is false in both manifest.json and command-evidence.json, and production_ready_candidate is only coverage.valid && slice.valid (descriptor validity), so readiness is not overstated; (3) no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced via frozen attestations (no_real_data, writes_product_state:false, executes_ui_runtime:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, cross_tenant_access_allowed:false) and per-row leak guards; (4) Claude remains read-only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false); (5) Hermes is used only for deterministic H10 review/evidence packets, not product runtime. Review-hardening posture is sound: readiness-report shows a passing auth preflight with valid JSON, forbidden_review_evidence blockers are wired in, and no premature or invalid claude-review-result.json exists (adjudication/construction-inspection/review-result are correctly produced post-review). One non-blocking P3 cosmetic labeling overlap was found in the harness-generated changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout. Note: validation command exit codes were taken from command-evidence.json (Bash is unavailable to a read-only reviewer) and are corroborated by source inspection of the validator, tests, and contract.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies pack artifact paths as both untracked implementation and unrelated dirty files
findings.details: The harness-generated scope artifact lists docs/closeout-packs/cp00-326/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md under untracked_implementation_files (lines 102-105) AND under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 107-113). The pack manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json lines 1702-1706) excludes those two and lists only the docx, the c-ldip invalid-attempt JSON, and workbook/. So the two artifacts classify the same paths differently. This is cosmetic: validation-report.json confirms active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted: [] (no required untracked implementation file was dropped from scope), and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-326/ is legitimately the pack's own new evidence directory. It does not affect the pack deliverables, the descriptor-only boundaries, or the closeout decision.
findings.recommendation: Tighten the review harness's unrelated_dirty_files_preserved semantics (e.g., exclude the active pack's own artifact directory and the planning handoff from the 'unrelated' bucket, or rename it to 'all_preserved_dirty_files') so the scope artifact and manifest agree. No change required to CP00-326 product code.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-326 intake core ui workflow slice descriptor pack
